title: Parkio Daily for June 13, 2026
teaser: Disneyland refurb calendar adds six summer closures; new Logo Mania merch and Celebrates America food drops today across both coasts.

bignews.title: Six new summer refurbishments added to Disneyland calendar
bignews.body: Disneyland just posted six additional attraction closures running through summer and fall. Multiple rides will go down for scheduled maintenance during what is typically the resort's busiest season.

parkioInsight.text: Check the official Disneyland app before rope drop to confirm your must-do rides are running. Summer closures often mean longer waits at similar attractions nearby.

bignews.title: Logo Mania merchandise collection debuts across Disney Parks
bignews.body: A new six-design collection celebrating favorite attractions, lands, and park elements is now available. The colorful line lets guests wear their allegiance to specific Disney experiences.

parkioInsight.text: Limited-run park merch often vanishes from flagship stores by mid-afternoon. Worth a stop at World of Disney or Emporium before 11 a.m. if you want full size selection.

bignews.title: Celebrates America food items roll out for 250th anniversary
bignews.body: Disney Chefs created red-white-and-blue treats across domestic parks, including Fourth of July beignets at Walt Disney World. The limited menu runs through early July to mark America's 250th birthday.

parkioInsight.text: Festival booths and bakery counters tend to sell out of specialty items by late afternoon. Grab patriotic snacks during your first park break if you want to try them.

bignews.title: Ghirardelli moving into temporary Disney Springs location
bignews.body: A new permit reveals the chocolate shop will occupy a popular Disney Springs storefront while work continues elsewhere. The interim space keeps the brand accessible to guests during construction.

parkioInsight.text: Temporary retail setups often mean shorter lines and quieter browsing. If you've been skipping Ghirardelli due to crowds, the new spot may offer easier access.

topstories.title: New baby animals arrive at Disney's Animal Kingdom
topstories.body: Spring births at the park include multiple species, with animal care teams celebrating milestones behind the scenes. The arrivals cap off a busy season for the conservation and care programs.

parkioInsight.text: Morning safari rides often give the best views of younger animals when they're most active. Book Kilimanjaro Safaris for the first two hours after park open if you want clearer sightings.

topstories.title: Cars 20th anniversary merchandise now at Walt Disney World
topstories.body: A racing jacket, apparel, and collectibles celebrating two decades of Pixar's Cars franchise are available in stores and online. The line includes checkered-flag details and character graphics.

parkioInsight.text: Anniversary collections typically see the fastest turnover at Hollywood Studios near Lightning McQueen's Racing Academy. Check there first if you're hunting specific sizes.

topstories.title: New EPCOT lounge opens with two exclusive snacks
topstories.body: A fresh lounge space debuted in EPCOT this week, offering two signature bites not available anywhere else in the park. Early reviews suggest the quality matches the setting.

parkioInsight.text: Lounges tend to fill up between 2 and 5 p.m. as guests seek air conditioning. Visiting just after park open or during evening shows usually means walk-in seating.

topstories.title: Cake Bake Shop adds three new desserts to bakery menu
topstories.body: The BoardWalk bakery introduced a trio of cakes priced around sixty dollars each. Reviews confirm the presentation and flavor justify the premium cost for special occasions.

parkioInsight.text: High-end bakery items often sell out before dinner service. If you're planning a celebration dessert, place an order or visit the counter before 3 p.m.

topstories.title: Pandora releases Moana jewelry collection ahead of live-action film
topstories.body: Disney Store launched a set of Moana charms and bracelets from Pandora, timed to the July 1 movie premiere. A free tote bag comes with any four-item purchase from the line.

parkioInsight.text: Film tie-in merch tends to disappear from park stores once the movie opens. Ordering online now or visiting World of Disney this weekend likely gives you the widest selection.

topstories.title: Multiple Bluey and Rapunzel LEGO sets now available online
topstories.body: New builds include Bluey's Grannies playset, a mini Rapunzel tower, and pizza-themed scenes. The sets range from 171 to several hundred pieces and are shipping now.

parkioInsight.text: LEGO exclusives often stock faster online than in park retail. If you're hunting a specific set, order ahead rather than counting on in-store inventory at Once Upon a Toy.

topstories.title: Lightning Lane Multi Pass strategy shifts at three Walt Disney World parks
topstories.body: Pre-arrival booking priorities and ride rankings changed this month at Magic Kingdom, EPCOT, and Hollywood Studios. The updates alter which attractions deliver the best skip-the-line value.

parkioInsight.text: Review your Lightning Lane selections the night before park day to confirm you're prioritizing the right tier-one rides. Strategy tweaks often mean different booking windows matter more now.

icymi.title: Indiana Jones 45th anniversary spirit jersey and props debut online
icymi.body: A weathered brown jersey and replica items from The Last Crusade arrived at Disney Store to celebrate Raiders of the Lost Ark's milestone. The collection honors the adventure franchise's legacy.

icymi.title: Epic Universe fireworks testing underway at Universal Orlando
icymi.body: Universal began trials of its first true nighttime spectacular at the new park. Launch sites installed over the past year are now active as the show moves toward debut.

icymi.title: Chris Sanders confirmed to direct Lilo and Stitch 2 live-action sequel
icymi.body: The original co-creator and voice of Stitch will helm the follow-up to the upcoming live-action film. Hollywood Reporter broke the news of Sanders' return to the franchise.

icymi.title: LEGOLAND Florida launches FIFA World Cup experience for summer
icymi.body: The park added soccer-themed activities and displays timed to the tournament. Fans can celebrate the World Cup with LEGO builds and interactive play zones through the season.

spotlight.title: Disney Springs hotels offer 25% teacher discount through September 7
spotlight.body: Resort Area Hotels at Disney Springs are running teacher appreciation rates with discounts up to 25 percent off best available pricing. The deal stacks with new dining and entertainment offers valid through early fall across the shopping and restaurant district.

parkioInsight.text: Teacher rates often sell out on weekend nights first. Booking mid-week stays now tends to lock in the discount before inventory tightens in July and August.
